body_weight.note: End of Legends of Mass Block A — ~6-7 lbs lean mass gained

body_weight_goals.note: End of year — lean and conditioned

prs.note: Confirmed single. Working sets were 315–325 at the time.
3x5 confirmed 3/22/2026. Strength maintained or progressed since fall 2025.
3 reps at 255. Working sets peaked around 245–250.
Peak working set. No max single on record. Current working set 265 lbs.
Peak working set weight reached Week 12.
Completed weighted sets. Currently limited by shoulder stability — no full extension.
Context-dependent. Standard working weight is 88 lb KBs.

pr_goals.note: Serious chase begins after Spartan and summer hypertrophy block.
Current PR is 255x3. Summer hypertrophy phase sets the base.

calf_health.notes: Right calf strain. Triggered by load stacking (run + heavy calf raises + explosive work same day). Not triggered by running alone. Currently at 1:3 walk/run ratio as of Week 3.

history.note: Burpees and explosive work caused flare. Stopped running.
The Pit — full calf rehab protocol, no running.
Run reintroduction started. 1:4 walk/run wks 1-2, 1:3 wk 3.

shoulder_stability.notes: Pull-ups limited by shoulder stability — cannot reach full extension. Dead hangs and scapular pull-ups introduced Week 3 of Operation Spartan to address.

history.note: Pull-up max was 5 reps at start of Legends of Mass.
5x5 pull-ups without full extension. Scapular pull-up protocol starting.

grip_endurance.notes: Farmer carries and monkey bars are primary grip builders. Grip-under-fatigue protocol introduced Week 3 of Operation Spartan.

history.note: Completed Samson Challenge rope pulls and carries.
88 lb KB farmer carries for 60 sec confirmed. Monkey bars improving.

running_endurance.notes: Reintroducing running after calf rehab. Gradual walk/run protocol.

timeline.label: Legends of Mass
timeline.subtitle: Powerbuilding — Strength & Size
timeline.summary: 8-week Block A + 8-week Block B. Full-body powerbuilding inspired by Alan Ritchson. Goal: +100 lbs on bench and squat from starting numbers.
highlights: Started at 233 lbs, 15% body fat
Gained ~6-7 lbs lean mass in Block A alone
Bench progressed from 195 to 245 lbs working sets
Squat progressed from 255 to 315+ lbs working sets
RDL reached 275 lbs working sets
Weighted pull-ups with 33 lb sandbag
Introduced barbell and landmine complexes
Pull-up max: 5 reps at start

timeline.label: Trial of Strength
timeline.subtitle: Samson Challenge Prep
timeline.summary: 4-week event-specific prep block for Samson Challenge. Strongman-style event practice: tire flips, rope pulls, sandbag runs, yoke carries, sled pulls, ground-to-overhead.
highlights: Tire flip simulations with heavy sled drags
Sandbag runs 3x200m @ 50 lbs
Rope pull practice and sled pulls up to 170 lb load
Yoke carry and overhead lunges
1000m row intervals at race pace
Taper week Sep 9-13

timeline.label: Samson Challenge
timeline.subtitle: Albuquerque, NM
timeline.summary: Multi-event strongman-style challenge. Events included 1K row, tire flips, rope pull, ground-to-overhead, sandbag run, sled pull, yoke carry, heavy bag over hurdle, overhead lunges, med ball toss, water wade.

timeline.label: Mass Ascension
timeline.subtitle: Powerbuilding Cut
timeline.summary: 12-week powerbuilding cut. Goal: reduce body fat while holding or building strength. 4 days/week. Calories reduced to 2,900-3,000. Strength continued climbing through Week 12.
highlights: Squat peaked at 365 lbs single (working sets 325-335)
Bench peaked at 255 lbs x3 (working sets 245-250)
RDL working sets reached 275 lbs
OHP working sets reached 150 lbs
Lat injury Week 5 — managed around it
Neck issue appeared mid-block
Calf began showing sensitivity to load stacking

timeline.label: The Pit
timeline.subtitle: Calf Rehab + Engine
timeline.summary: Recovery and conditioning block following calf strain and load stacking sensitivity identified in Mass Ascension. No running. 4 days/week focused on calf rehab, aerobic base, and strength maintenance. Ended with a ski trip.
highlights: Full calf rehab protocol — seated raises, isometrics, foam roll
Aerobic base via rowing and stair stepper
Strength maintained: squat, RDL, pulls
Zero explosive lower body work
Confirmed: load stacking (run + calf raises + explosive work) is the trigger — not running alone
Ended mid-February for ski trip

timeline.label: Operation Spartan
timeline.subtitle: 10-Week Race Prep
timeline.summary: 10-week Spartan Super 10K preparation program. Gradual calf-safe run reintroduction, obstacle-specific conditioning, grip endurance, shoulder stability work. Strength maintenance at 335 lb squat working sets.
highlights: Week 3 Day 1 confirmed: Squat 335, RDL 265, BSS 135 lb barbell
Run progression: 1:4 → 1:3 → ... → 30 min continuous
Burpee reintroduction: step version weeks 3-6, full week 7+
Dead hang and scapular pull-up program for shoulder stability
Landmine and barbell complex circuits for conditioning variety
Race goal: finish the Spartan Super 10K strong

timeline.label: Spartan Super 10K
timeline.subtitle: Colorado Springs, CO
timeline.summary: Spartan Super obstacle race. ~10K distance, 20+ obstacles. Elevation: ~6,000 ft. Obstacles include rope climbs, monkey bars, sandbag carries, barbed wire crawls, spear throw, and more.

timeline.label: Ritchson Phase
timeline.subtitle: Sarcoplasmic Hypertrophy
timeline.summary: Use the strength base built over the past year to produce visible muscle development. Higher reps (8-15), more volume, shorter rest, full-body days. Inspired by Alan Ritchson's training approach. Keep aerobic base from Spartan — running as a weekly habit.
highlights: Leverage 365 lb squat base for high-rep work
Target: visible definition from existing muscle
Full-body sessions, multiple compound movements
Maintain running 1-2x per week for conditioning
Sarcoplasmic focus — muscle fullness and size

timeline.label: Strength Conversion
timeline.subtitle: Myofibrillar Strength
timeline.summary: Convert summer hypertrophy gains into dense, functional strength. Return to lower reps (3-6), heavier loads, longer rest. Primary goals: squat 405 single, bench 315 single.
highlights: Chase squat 405 lb single
Chase bench 315 lb single
Myofibrillar focus — strength density
Lower rep ranges, higher intensity
